_comment: To add a new presentation, upload the PDF to assets/presentations/ and add a corresponding entry in the presentations array.

presentations.title: Safety-Aware Performance Boosting for Constrained Nonlinear Systems
presentations.description: This talk presents a learning-based control approach for constrained nonlinear systems that improves closed-loop performance while preserving safety guarantees. The method combines a stabilizing safety mechanism with an adaptive performance layer, ensuring constraint satisfaction and stability even as the controller exploits learned behavior online.

presentations.title: Stability-Preserving Online Adaptation of Neural Closed-loop Maps
presentations.description: This talk presents a framework for safely adapting neural closed-loop maps online while preserving stability guarantees. The approach enables neural controllers to improve or adjust their behavior during operation without compromising the certified stability properties of the closed-loop system.

presentations.title: Homothetic tube model predictive control with multi-step predictors
presentations.description: This talk presents a tube-based model predictive control framework for uncertain systems using multi-step predictors. The approach combines homothetic tube parametrizations with learned or data-driven prediction models to reduce conservatism while maintaining robust constraint satisfaction.

presentations.title: ADMM vs Contract Based Methods for distributed MPC
presentations.description: This talk presents distributed MPC methods for mobile multi-agent systems operating under time-varying communication and safety constraints. It compares iterative ADMM-based optimization with contract-based approaches, showing how multi-agent teams can safely handle plug-and-play operations, collision avoidance, and connectivity maintenance in changing networks.

presentations.title: Model Predictive Control for constrained navigation of autonomous vehicles
presentations.event: IEEE ITSS Italian Chapter Annual Meeting
presentations.description: This talk presents model predictive control methods for constrained navigation of autonomous vehicles in partially unknown environments. It focuses on how multi-trajectory MPC can balance safety and performance by combining an exploiting trajectory for task execution with a safe trajectory that guarantees obstacle avoidance and recursive feasibility.
